headline_risk_context.headline: Markets reacted to a fragile US-Iran ceasefire (oil down, risk-on) while Fed minutes introduced renewed hawkish uncertainty on yields

supporting_metrics.us_treasury_10y_snapshot_note: minutes signaled some openness to hikes — yields mixed

notes: Major wire services reported a temporary/truce-related relief rally and oil volatility on 2026-04-08/09; Reuters also published Fed minutes commentary on 2026-04-08 that tempered parts of the rally due to possible hawkish tilt.
These narratives are supporting context for Taiwan's pre-open risk appetite.

market_driver_summary.primary_driver: Fragile US-Iran ceasefire & near-term oil-price moves (risk-on impulse)
secondary_drivers: Federal Reserve minutes showing some openness to rate hikes (Treasury yield sensitivity)
Ongoing trade/tariff policy uncertainty (administration-level moves earlier in April)
market_driver_summary.driver_direction: risk_on (equity-positive) with conditional risk from yields/FX
likely_affected_groups: Large-cap export-oriented tech & semiconductor names (TSMC, suppliers)
Cyclical exporters and ETFs sensitive to foreign flows
Oil & energy-linked names (indirect, commodity-negative if oil falls)
FX-sensitive sectors if USD/TWD moves materially
market_driver_summary.why_it_matters: A ceasefire that eases Middle East risk lowers oil risk premia and can lift global risk appetite, prompting foreign net-buying into Taiwan equities; however, Fed minutes that hint at potential hiking bias could push yields higher and cap upside—so today's net direction depends on persistence of the ceasefire and near-term yield moves.
market_driver_summary.what_would_invalidate: Ceasefire breakdown (oil spike) or a clear hawkish shift in Fed communications that meaningfully lifts US Treasury yields; large negative earnings or Taiwan-specific policy shocks would also invalidate the risk-on read.

persistent_watch: US monetary policy path & Treasury yields (10y moves)
Middle East geopolitics (ceasefire durability & oil price trajectory)
Semiconductor / AI earnings & guidance (TSMC, Nvidia supply-chain signals)
Trade/tariff policy headlines affecting Asia supply chains
USD/TWD and DXY moves (capital flow sensitivity)

current_high_priority.title: US-Iran ceasefire & oil-price volatility
current_high_priority.why: Primary near-term narrative that lifted risk appetite on 2026-04-08/09; drives foreign flows and regional equities
current_high_priority.expected_actionability: High — index-level and sector rotation implications

current_high_priority.title: Fed minutes (March) signalling openness to hikes
current_high_priority.why: Could cap the relief rally by lifting yields; direct influence on USD and rates-sensitive sectors

current_high_priority.title: TWSE institutional flow (foreign buy/sell on 2026-04-09)
current_high_priority.why: Concrete onshore flow that validates whether risk-on is accompanied by real foreign buying

deprioritized.title: April tariff shock narratives
deprioritized.why: Still an important structural risk but immediate market action has been dominated by geopolitics and yields in the last 3–5 days; downgrade until fresh tariff headlines emerge

surprise_buckets: Ceasefire breakdown → oil spike → rapid risk-off reversal
Unexpected hawkish Fed communication → yield spike & equity rotation
Major semiconductor earnings or supply-chain shock → sector-led sell-off
Sharp USD/TWD move driven by FX or policy surprise
